DataTypeStruct.Description: バージョン（Morrowindの場合は1.2、BloodmoonおよびTribunalの場合は1.3）

Flags.Description: ファイルは、ファイル拡張子に関係なく、マスターとして扱う必要があります

GroupFields.Description: 以前のマスターファイルのサイズ（バイト単位）（プラグインのバージョン追跡に使用）

フィールドタイプ（s = short、l = long、f = float）
指定されたタイプに関係なく全ての値はfloatとして格納される。

DataTypeStruct.Description: マイナー/メジャースキルを交互に5セット。

Fields.Description: ランク名。エントリは常に10個あるが必須ではない。

DataTypeStruct.Description: Skills - use Skill values found on Skills, ignoring any value of -1.

GroupFields.Description:
Faction name string
An ANAM/INTV pair occurs for each faction with a reaction adjustment. Multiple entries with the same faction exist in some records, but only the first appears to be used by the game.
Faction reaction value (normally -3 to +3; other values exist and are allowed in the CS, but reportedly may generate errors)

DataTypeStruct.Description: Strength (for all fields until flags: first is male, second is female)

Fields.Description: Casting visual (note that Casting visual comes before Bolt visual, unlike with sounds)

List of all the local script variables separated by NULL characters
(varCount = NumShorts + NumLongs + NumFloats from SCHD)
Weather chances - 8 bytes in v1.2 (Morrowind), 10 bytes in v1.3 (Bloodmoon/Tribunal)
Index (although nominally a uint32, uint16s are used as indices in LAND records, so these are effectively restricted to uint16 values)

DataTypeStruct.Description: Unknown (appears to be unused - only values of 0 and 1 are seen)

GroupFields.Description: Cell name for previous DODT, if interior

DataTypeStruct.Description: Skills (in order of Skill ID, as found on Morrowind:Skills)

GroupFields.Description: Cell name for previous DODT, if interior

Biped object (Note: The only field size currently in use is a uint8, but the internal coding allows for a uint32 here, using only the lowest byte of it. This may be from an earlier file format that's no longer used.)
Male armor name (optional, but present in all base game files)
Female armor name (optional, male armor is used if not present)

Biped object (Note: The only field size currently in use is a uint8, but the internal coding allows for a uint32 here, using only the lowest byte of it. This may be from an earlier file format that's no longer used.)
Male armor name (optional, but present in all base game files)
Female armor name (optional, male armor is used if not present)

Flags.Description: カルシネーター（か焼器）

Flags.Description: Calculate from all levels <= PC's level
Calculate for each item in count
Calculate from all levels <= PC's level

Fields.Description: Cell Name. Unlike other NAME fields, this is the localized, human-readable name of the cell, not a language-agnostic ID string. Exterior regions are mostly empty strings; for these, the region name is used in the Construction Set.
Count of Temporary Children (Note: conceivably, this might not match the actual number of objects.)

References.Description: These only appear in mod files when creatures or NPCs are moved from one cell to another; they commonly appear in saved game files as things move around.

Fields.Description: Reference ID (always the same as the attached FRMR value)
Name of the cell the reference was moved to (interior cells only)
Coordinates of the cell the reference was moved to (exterior cells only)
Reference to the form that was moved

References.Description: These only appear in mod files when creatures or NPCs are moved from one cell to another; they commonly appear in saved game files as things move around.

Fields.Description: Reference blocked (value is always 0; present if Blocked is set in the reference's record header, otherwise absent)
Reference's scale, if applicable and not 1.0

GroupFields.Description: Faction ID (not light, NPC, or static)

Fields.Description: ID of soul in gem (soul gems only)
Enchantment charge (charged items with non-zero charges)

Remaining usage
uint32 - health remaining (weapons and armor)
uint32 - uses remaining (locks, probes, repair items)
float32 - time remaining (lights)

GroupFields.Description: Cell name for previous DODT, if interior

Fields.Description: Reference is disabled (always 0). Like UNAM, this will be emitted if the relevant flag is set in the reference's record header. This may only be possible via scripting. Also, even if present in the file, the field appears to be ignored on loading.
Data types included. If the relevant bit isn't set, the related fields will not be loaded, even if present.

Vertex Normals. A 65×65 array of:
Note that the Y-direction of the data is from the bottom up.

DataTypeStruct.Description: A height offset for the entire cell. Decreasing this value will shift the entire cell land down (by 8 units).[verification needed — Oblivion and Skyrim use 8 units, is this the same?]

Contains the height data for the cell in the form of a 65×65 pixel array. The height data is not absolute values but uses differences between adjacent pixels. Thus a pixel value of 0 means it has the same height as the last pixel. Note that the Y-direction of the data is from the bottom up.

Fields.Description: Heights for world map. Derived from VHGT data.
Vertex Colors. A 65×65 array of RGB (no alpha) values.
Texture Indices. Each value corresponds to the Index (INTV) value from a LTEX record.

Note: the Construction Set allows for a 44-byte version of the structure where NAME is a 32-byte null-terminated string immediately after Grid Y, rather than a field of its own. Only the 12-byte version is currently in use; the 44-byte version appears to be an older version.
The cell name the path grid belongs to.

DataTypeStruct.Description: Connection count (number of entries in PGRC)

Fields.Description:
Connection list (also known as edges), where TotalConnCount is the sum of all Connection count values in the PGRP node.
For each path point listed in PGRP, there are Connection count edges in this list, each an index into PGRP. Using Azura's Coast (12, 20) as an example, the first entry in the PGRP (index 0) the Connection count value is 1, so you would read one uint32 from this list. That has a value of 1, so index 0 connects to index 1. For the second entry (index = 1), Connection count is 4, so you would read the next four uint32 values: 0, 6, 3, and 2, meaning that the point at index 1 connects to the points at indices 0, 6, 3, and 2.

ID - this appears to be generated from the creature name (or DEFAULT) combined with the type formatted as a four-digit number with leading zeroes (e.g., the ID for an alit scream is alit0006).
Note: there is one record in Bloodmoon which has an empty string here (BM_horker, Left Foot).
Info name string (unique sequence of #'s), ID

DataTypeStruct.Description: Dialogue type (duplicates parent DIAL's DATA value)

Fields.Description:
Info data (always emitted except for deleted records)
Note: a 20-byte version is also allowed in the Construction Set, but does not appear in any game files. It is likely an older version. The data appeared in the same order, but all values were int32/uint32.
Faction (or the literal string "FFFF" for _NO FACTION_)

Flags.Description: Global/Local/Not Local: Value is a float in FLTV
Global/Local/Not Local: Value is a long in INTV
Global/Local/Not Local: Value is a short in INTV (serves only as a value range - INTV is always a uint32)

DataTypeStruct.Description: Name: Except for functions (which require no further information), everything after the fifth character is the ID for the global, local, journal, etc.

Fields.Description: Script name (technically optional, but internal conditions seem like they should always be satisfied)
